description: Migration up adds a nullable default_contact_id column to rmm_organization_mappings
FK rejects a contact from another tenant/client and SET NULLs default_contact_id when the contact is deleted
Index (tenant, default_contact_id) is created
Migration down removes the column, FK, and index
up -> down -> up round-trip succeeds (idempotent)
Resolver returns the mapping contact when it is active and belongs to the client
Resolver ignores an inactive mapping contact and falls back to the client default
Resolver ignores a mapping contact belonging to a different client and falls back
Resolver falls back to clients.properties.primary_contact_id when no mapping contact is set
Resolver returns null when the fallback primary contact is inactive or cross-client
Resolver returns null when neither a mapping contact nor a client primary contact exists
Resolver reads rows written earlier in the same transaction (uses the passed trx)
createTicketForAlert sets contact_name_id from mappingDefaultContactId
createTicketForAlert sets contact_name_id from the client default when the mapping contact is unset
createTicketForAlert leaves contact_name_id null when no contact resolves
processRmmAlertEvent threads the org mapping default_contact_id through to the created ticket
createTicketForAlertId threads the org mapping default_contact_id through to the created ticket
Ticket numbering still uses generate_next_number after the contact change (no regression)
createHuntressTicket sets contact_name_id from defaultContactId
createHuntressTicket falls back to the client default contact
incidentProcessor passes mapping.default_contact_id to createHuntressTicket
Unmapped incident (fallback/triage client) resolves no mapping contact and creates the ticket without error
Huntress create publishes TICKET_CREATED with tenantId and ticketId after commit
Shared processRmmAlertEvent publishes TICKET_CREATED on the create path
createTicketForAlertId publishes TICKET_CREATED after its transaction
TICKET_CREATED is NOT published on the append-note / status-update path
A failing event publish is swallowed and the ticket is still created/committed
Integration: TICKET_CREATED -> ticketEmailSubscriber sends the client 'Ticket Created' email when a contact resolved
Existing RMM alert pipeline integration test stays green
getHuntressOrganizationMappings returns default_contact_id
updateHuntressOrganizationMapping persists a set default_contact_id
updateHuntressOrganizationMapping clears default_contact_id to null
getNinjaOneOrganizationMappings returns default_contact_id
updateNinjaOneOrganizationMapping sets and clears default_contact_id
Update actions still enforce existing permission/tier gating
Huntress manager renders a Default Contact picker per mapping row
NinjaOne manager renders a Default Contact picker per mapping row
Picker options are limited to the mapped client's contacts
Picker is disabled when the row has no mapped client
Selecting a contact calls the update action with default_contact_id
Clearing the contact calls the update action with null
Saved default contact is reflected after reload
